rag_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers or invalid types should be resolved by checking visible AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex, or removing the invalid clause.
AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and types from the current AADL scope; avoid introducing package-qualified or undefined references.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the actual expression types (bool, int, real) and avoid unsafe conversions.
generic_rules: Repair actions should target only the specific diagnostic issue without altering unrelated architecture elements.
AGREE clauses must be syntactically valid and semantically consistent with the AADL model's visible features and types.
If a clause references an undefined or mismatched type, either correct the reference or remove the clause to maintain model integrity.
Avoid introducing new identifiers or types that are not present in the current AADL scope or its configured dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Helper declarations in AGREE must match the type of the expressions they define (e.g., bool, int, real).
Boolean comparisons should only be used with actual Boolean ports or valid AGREE bool variables.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to fix diagnostics by introducing undefined or package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data types into integers or numeric comparisons unless the AADL model explicitly supports such representations.
Do not add new clauses or modify architecture beyond the scope of the reported diagnostic.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
Component type annexes vs implementation annexes (not relevant to repair scope)
